Title: Public sex offenders registry still under discussion - Nancy
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Relevant ministries are still in discussions on establishing a public sex offenders registry, says Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri.
She said setting up the registry is a complex task involving various legal and administrative hurdles.
"We are working towards establishing a registry for individuals involved in such offences, but it is not easy.
"We have held discussions with various ministries. At present, no concrete decision has been reached. It is not that there is no support, but there are procedural matters that need to be addressed.
"It is not easy, as there are also human rights considerations. So far, nothing has been finalised, but we are continuing efforts to move this forward.
"We will do our best to ensure we achieve what we intend to do for the sake of children's rights," she told reporters after the "A Journey of Malaysia's Participation under the Convention on the Rights of the Child" event today.
Nancy was responding to a question on whether the ministry would push for a public registry of sexual offenders, following an increase in cases of child sexual abuse, including online offences.
Currently, the Sexual Crime Registry System (eDKK), introduced in 2019, is administered by the Social Welfare Department.
However, the registry is not publicly accessible in the same way as in the United States, where members of the public can search for individuals by first and last name or conduct location-based checks.
Additionally, Nancy added that amendments to the Child Act 2001 are also underway to strengthen protections for children, including against online threats.
Meanwhile, Nancy expressed concern over a sexual assault case involving a teacher against her 14-year-old male student since last year.
"We hope that the ministry or whichever agency is concerned and responsible for, apart from D11, even the ministry where she's (the teacher) working under, will take stricter action."
"I hope that they do not put the case aside and just keep that person in the icebox. They have to do something because this is not very good for the child," she said.
On Wednesday, a teacher was charged at the Sessions Court in Seremban, Negri Sembilan, with
five counts
of committing sexual assault against her 14-year-old male student since last year.
The 42-year-old woman, however, pleaded not guilty to all charges read before Judge Siti Aishah Ab Rashid.
Meanwhile, at the event, Nancy also launched a book titled The Continuing Covenant: Malaysia's Journey Under the Convention on the Rights of the Child.
She said the publication reflected Malaysia's 30-year journey under the Convention on the Rights of the Child (CRC) framework and the country's commitment to protecting children's rights.
She added Malaysia's recent engagement in Geneva under the CRC reflected months of technical coordination and policy consolidation involving multiple agencies.
"It reaffirmed the progress we have achieved in strengthening systems related to protection, welfare, education, health and justice," she said.
Nancy said discussions in Geneva highlighted the need for protection systems to evolve in addressing emerging challenges affecting children.
She added that the ministry's priorities were strengthening digital safety, addressing youth mental health issues, and ensuring support for vulnerable groups, including children with disabilities, those in alternative care, and children affected by displacement or documentation issues.
"Our guiding principle is that policies must translate into meaningful improvements for children in the most vulnerable situations," she said.
Nancy added that protecting children required cooperation between governments, civil society groups, and international partners.
At the event, she also announced Datuk Yasmeen Shariff as Malaysia's candidate to the United Nations Committee on the Rights of the Child 2027-2031.
She said Yasmeen's experience in humanitarian affairs and international cooperation reflected Malaysia's commitment to the multilateral human rights system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Geng Hadi' busted over motorcycle thefts in Sentul
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A motorcycle theft syndicate known as "Geng Hadi", believed to have been active since January, was busted after police arrested all six members in separate raids around Kepong on Saturday.
Sentul deputy police chief Superintendent Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 15 and 33, were believed to have stolen motorcycles for spare parts.
He said the syndicate leader, identified only as Hadi, 18, who is unemployed, was believed to have dismantled the stolen motorcycles before disposing of their frames into a river.
"Police received a report from a 55-year-old local man working as a courier, who claimed that his Honda EX5 motorcycle had been stolen on May 13 at Flat Mutiara, Taman Fadason.
"Following the report, the Sentul district police headquarters' D4 Criminal Investigation Division conducted intelligence gathering and successfully arrested the six suspects while also seizing two motorcycles," he told reporters today.
Jasni said police believed the arrest of all six suspects, including the syndicate mastermind, had helped solve at least eight motorcycle theft cases in the Sentul district.
Checks revealed that all suspects had previous criminal records under Section 379A of the Penal Code, while urine tests conducted on them returned negative results.
"Further investigations led police to recover five incomplete motorcycle frames from a river, along with a motorcycle cover set and two mobile phones seized from the suspects.
"All seized items were brought to the Sentul district police headquarters and the arrests have helped solve eight motorcycle theft cases around Sentul, involving estimated losses of about RM20,000," he said.
Jasni added that all suspects, including the underage suspect who had dropped out of school, were remanded from May 17 until today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Health Ministry to expand treatment scope of private clinics to empower GPs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry (MoH) plans to expand the scope of treatments at private clinics as part of measures to improve existing policies to empower general practitioners (GP).
The MoH said the measures include allowing services such as ultrasound and Occupational Health Doctor (OHD) services offered effectively and safely.
"The step is taken to acknowledge the competencies and additional training by GPs.
"The MoH is reviewing policy improvements by drafting implementation directives for specific service scopes at private clinics," the ministry said.
It was previously reported that there was a decline in patient numbers following the growth of general practitioners. Not only that, the surge of medication costs has caused a large portion of the country's 10,000 private clinics to face difficulties in continuing operations.
In fact, over 70 per cent of the GP clinics identified were found to earn an income below RM60,000 per month, causing them to struggle in keeping operations afloat. The findings were identified by the Malaysian Medication Association (MMA) in a study involving 2,000 clinics nationwide.
MMA President Datuk Dr R Thirunavukarasu called on the health ministry to ease the congestion at government clinics by referring basic healthcare cases to GP clinics to assist them and revitalise their services.
As part of the its improvement efforts, the second measure currently pursued by the ministry includes expanding the scope of Skim Peduli Kesihatan untuk Kumpulan B40 (PEKA B40) and the Madani Medical Scheme programmes to include treatments for non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
However, it said the expansion of the programme is subject to approval of future financial allocations.
PEKA B40 and the Madani Medical Scheme are private-public partnership models designed specifically as a collaborative platform for private GPs.
The Madani Medical Scheme focused on acute primary outpatient care or minor illnesses such as fever, flu, cough, diarrhea, and food poisoning.
Meanwhile, PEKA B40 includes screening and early detection of NCDs such as diabetes, high blood pressure and high cholesterol.
According to the ministry, the third measure currently pursued is a collaboration between the Finance Ministry (MoF) and the Employee Provident Fund (EPF) as well as private consultants to review and improve its system and service delivery aspects in processing applications for the opening of private clinics.
The MoH is currently implementing a more integrated approach to strengthen broader governance coordination in relation to the country's primary healthcare sector including the revitalisation of private GPs.
"This approach is intended to optimise the capacity and overall potential of the national healthcare system, including cooperation between the private and public sectors towards ensuring an equitable, attainable and quality healthcare for all.
"Strategic cooperation between the government and private GP has long existed through public healthcare programmes such as vaccinations, health screening, all done under the PEKA B40 and Madani Medical Scheme.
"This demonstrates that the involvement of the private sector truly helps to increase access to services, especially to targeted groups," he says.
The MoH also said an inclusive and sustainable long-term reform requires a detailed and holistic focus, especially with the current legal aspects, service execution services, operation mechanism, monetary mechanism and procurement procedures.
It requires continued engagements with all stakeholders to ensure all proposals will be executed effectively, fairly and beneficial to the public, the ministry said.
"The MoH is confident in the commitment to strengthen the primary healthcare system through careful planning and phase-by-phase execution in line with the national healthcare reform agenda," they said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Clothing brand founder, six others fined over Bukit Bintang flare incident
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Seven men, including a local clothing brand founder, pleaded guilty in the magistrates' courts here to multiple offences linked to a viral publicity stunt involving flares and reckless driving in Bukit Bintang last weekend.
The accused, aged between 22 and 31, admitted to multiple criminal charges after they were read before three separate magistrates' courts here today.
Mohamad Azhar Mohamed Idris, 31, was fined RM4,500, in default six months' jail, for stopping a vehicle in a dangerous manner at a traffic light junction along Jalan Sultan Ismail and Jalan Bukit Bintang at 2.07am on May 18.
He was charged under Section 48(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, which provides for a maximum fine of RM5,000 or imprisonment of up to one year, or both.
Five of the accused, namely, Amir Adzim Shahrul Nizam, 25; Khairul Hamizan Zainudin, 28; Muhammad Izz Mifzal Mohd Noordzlie, 22; Muhammad Ariff Aiman Mohamed Nasir, 23; and Muhammad Ikhwan Daniel Azman, 24, pleaded guilty before magistrate Aina Azahra Arifin to committing public nuisance at Shibuya Junction in Bukit Bintang at about 2am on May 18.
They were each fined RM400 under Section 268 of the Penal Code.
Meanwhile, Muhammad Haziq Akmal Mohd Yazid, 24, was fined RM50 for igniting a flare, which prosecutors said posed a danger to public safety, at the same location and time.
The offence was charged under Section 4 of the Minor Offences Act, which carries a maximum RM50 fine.
The prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutors Hench Go, Iman Nurhidayah Ezam and Nurbatrisyia Mohd Razib, while lawyers Khairul Muktar and Simret Singh represented all the accused.
Go said the group had travelled in a pickup truck and on electric scrambler motorcycles while igniting flares during the promotional stunt, which was later uploaded on social media and went viral.
"This was a very dangerous stunt carried out on a busy street. The court must impose a deterrent sentence," he said.
Khairul, when appealing for Amir Adzim, the owner of the local clothing brand, pleaded for a lenient sentence on the grounds that his client was remorseful and had given full cooperation throughout the investigation.
The court was told the incident stemmed from a promotional video shoot for the brand's anniversary in Bukit Bintang, which went viral after footage showed individuals setting off flares atop a moving vehicle and riding motorcycles dangerously in the busy entertainment district.
Police had earlier
detained 15 people
in connection with the May 18 incident.
Initial investigations revealed the clothing store involved was owned by a 24-year-old man operating in Dengkil, while a 24-year-old woman acted as the coordinator for the shoot.
The case had drawn public attention after videos circulated widely on social media showing reckless behaviour in the heart of the capital's nightlife area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Myanmar man beaten to death after drunken dispute in Kepong
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A misunderstanding fuelled by alcohol among three Myanmar citizens ended in tragedy when one of them was beaten to death beneath the Metro Prima Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) station bridge in Kepong on Sunday.
Sentul deputy police chief Superintendent Jasni Zolpa said police received a report from a member of the public at 1.40pm regarding an unconscious 39-year-old man at the location.
Following the report, a team from the Sentul district police headquarters criminal investigation division launched an operation around Kepong at about 12.30pm on Monday.
"The operation led to the arrest of two Myanmar men and a Myanmar woman aged between 30 and 40 to assist in investigations," he told reporters today.
Jasni said preliminary investigations revealed that the incident was believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding between the victim and the suspects, which escalated into a fight.
"All the suspects have been remanded for seven days from May 19 until May 25 to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Farm Fresh posts lower Q4 earnings, may hike select Malaysia and Singapore product prices
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Farm Fresh Bhd's net profit slightly declined to RM27.9 million in the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2024 (Q4 FY26) from RM28.35 million a year ago.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia the company said the decline was due to higher distribution costs resulting from increased school milk sales, accompanied with the freight costs arising from exports to Cambodia and Philippines.
Earnings for the quarter was also affected by higher salary costs due to increased headcount to support new Inside Scoop outlet openings and business expansion.
However, revenue for the quarter under review rose 12.9 per cent to RM275.12 million from RM243.73 million in Q3 FY25 driven by increase in revenue from its Malaysian operations fueled by higher mini market, e-commerce and school milk sales.
Additionally, higher exports to Cambodia, stronger sales in Philippines and the positive sales contribution from launching of new products such as Butter, AusFresh, and Farm Fresh Full Cream Milk Powder further contributed to the growth.
For the full financial year, Farm Fresh's earnings climbed to RM129.61 million from RM106.4 million a year ago while revenue rose to RM1.12 billion from RM981.18 million.
Looking ahead, the company said it sent notices of price increase of about three per cent for the selected plastic bottles products in Malaysia to curb rise in cost due to the Middle East war.
While in Singapore, Farm Fresh increased the price by about 10 per cent.
The price increases will be affected by early June 2026.
The company said that rapid escalation of hostilities in the Middle East has introduced major risks to global economic stability, particularly due to the disruption of shipping lanes essential to international trade in general and specifically to Farm Fresh, supply of plastic bottles.
The blockade on Strait of Hormuz has resulted in both a severe shortage of supply and also significant increase in prices of plastic bottles, due to unavailability of high-density polyethylene (HDPE) resin, that relies on naphtha shipped from the Middle East.
"This affected the supply of our flagship two-litre and one-litre fresh milk in plastic bottles. We have taken several steps to address this, which has resolved both the availability and cost pressure resulting from this situation," it said.
Among other measures taken by the company to address the issues include increasing its gable top paper cartons production to make up for the lack of plastic bottles.
It also worked with its plastic bottle suppliers to source HDPE resin from China, which has restored Farm Fresh's plastic bottle supplies to pre-war levels beginning May 2026, albeit at higher prices.
"Going into the next financial year, there are several headwinds that might impact us, in particular those related to the Middle East war, that might impact us in terms of logistics and costs.
"With that in mind, we are cautiously optimistic to continue to deliver both tangible progress in terms of our category expansion and regional expansion and recording better financial performance,"  said Farm Fresh.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: IWK award reflects Malaysia's sustainability commitment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the international recognition received by Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) reflects the country's commitment to strengthening sustainable water and sanitation management.
Anwar, who is also finance minister, said efforts to reinforce this are in line with the 2030 Sustainable Development Agenda (SDGs).
"May this success continue to inspire efforts to build a more sustainable, inclusive and prosperous nation for future generations," he said on social media today.
The national sewerage company won the SDG6 Award at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid, Spain, on Tuesday.
The award recognises the company's submission, 'Solar-powered Sanitation: No Upfront Cost, Cuts Emissions', which demonstrates how the use of renewable energy can enhance wastewater treatment operational performance, drive sustainable infrastructure development and strengthen financial resilience.
The award-winning programme involves the implementation of solar energy systems across 16 sewage treatment plants nationwide through a Private Financing Initiative (PFI) model.
The initiative generates a solar energy capacity of 6.3 megawatt-peak (MWp) and produces approximately 7,700 megawatt-hours (MWh) of renewable energy annually to support the treatment of nearly 148 million cubic metres of wastewater each year.
Furthermore, the initiative is expected to save nearly RM41.8 million in electricity costs from the grid, while reducing more than 5 gigagrams (Gg) of carbon dioxide emissions annually.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alliance Bank logs in 10.1pct higher net profit  to RM826.53mil in FY26
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd's net profit rose 10.1 per cent to RM826.53 million in the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26) from RM750.53 million.
The bank's revenue grew 8.65 per cent to RM2.47 billion from RM2.27 billion.
According to Alliance Bank, the higher revenue was due to the growth in both net interest income (NII) and non-interest income (NOII).
Its NII of RM2.01 billion was driven primarily by higher loan volumes with net interest margin (NIM) standing at 2.34 per cent. NOII jumped 42 per cent year-on-year (YoY) to RM459.2 million.
The bank's FY26 cost-to-income stood at 47.9 per cent amid continued investments in people and technology.
On the balance sheet, the bank's gross loans expanded 7.5 per cent YoY, supported by broad-based expansion across small and medium enterprises SME, commercial and consumer segments.
Customer deposits grew 8.8 per cent YoY, supported mainly by fixed deposit growth, while the bank maintained a current account savings account (Casa) ratio of 37.5 per cent.
Alliance Bank chief executive officer Kellee Kam said the bank's FY26 performance reflects the disciplined execution of its Acceler8 strategy and the resilience of its diversified business model.
"As we navigate global uncertainties, we are focused on leveraging our solid financial foundation to stand by our customers, support their transition journeys, and deliver responsible, long-term value to all stakeholders," said Kam.
For the fourth quarter, Alliance Bank's net profit rose 4.33 per cent to RM206.04 million from RM197.49 million a year ago, while revenue grew 5.3 per cent to RM593.08 million from RM563.24 million.
The bank declared a second interim dividend of 9.74 sen per share, bringing total FY26 dividends to 19.1 sen per share.
Meanwhile, Alliance Bank is is stepping up support for businesses and individuals amid geopolitical uncertainties and a challenging operating environment.
The bank has joined Bank Negara Malaysia's SME Stabilisation Relief Facility to provide financing support for SMEs and micro-enterprises affected by the ongoing Middle East conflict.
The initiative complements Alliance Bank's existing SME solutions, including collateral-free working capital financing of up to RM500,000 under SME Express Financing.
The bank said it will also continue working closely with affected customers on repayment flexibility and other relief measures where necessary.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zara always slept in class, Coroner's court told
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The late Zara Qairina Mahathir often slept in class, according to her classmates, dormmates and teacher.
Consultant Forensic Psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung said if he remembered correctly, she was even described as one of the students who slept the most in class.
"According to most of her friends, there was no change in those sleeping patterns that showed she had insufficient sleep during the night.
"This is something which was not apparent during primary school years," the 67th witness testified before coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 72nd inquest into the child's death.
When replying to lawyer Datuk Rizwandean M.Borhan, Dr Chua said he is aware of a school warden's testimony that Zara Qairina had slept less in the class after first semester examination.
However, the expert said his statement of the child's chronic insufficient sleep was mostly based on friends and classmates who spent more time with her.
Rizwandean: Yesterday, I mentioned about (Zara Qairina's) disappointment over the Badar issue (prefect) and chronic insufficient sleep. These two findings are merely based on your assumption because you want to list out as many risk factors as possible to fit in your narrative that Zara had commited suicide?
Dr Chua: I disagree, these are clinical formulations based on psychological constructs, taking into consideration the neurobiological aspect. Again, psychology is deeply personal and contextual, interpretation needed to be based on that individual.
Rizwandean also examined the reasons on why Dr Chua only highlighted the negative parts of Zara Qairina's life in his autopsy report.
The witness said report was written looking into the psychological construct of Zara Qairina, to understand the risk factors.
"I do acknowledge that there were normal expressions of an adolescent along the way in the diaries."
Rizwandean is representing Zara Qairina's mother, Noraidah Lamat.
Inquest will resume on June 22.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Food company fined RM21,000 for falsifying halal logo
Article:
KUALA KANGSAR: A peanut-based food company was fined RM21,000 by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to falsifying the use of a halal logo on its product packaging last year.
Mee Hiong Yuen Groundnut Factory Sdn Bhd, represented by director Wong Hoong Kit, 64, entered the plea before judge Nuur Hafiedzah Md Daud.
Wong was found to have falsely described seven "Pemancing" peanut product packages as halal, in contravention of Paragraph 4(1) of the Trade Descriptions (Certification and Marking of Halal) Order 2011.
The offence was committed at the company's premises in Jalan Besar Sungai Siput (U), Sungai Siput, at 11am on Nov 3, 2025.
The offence falls under Paragraph 8(a) of the same order and is punishable under Paragraph 8(A), which carries a maximum fine of RM200,000 and up to RM500,000 for a second or subsequent offence upon conviction.
The prosecution was conducted by Perak Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry prosecuting officer Muhamad Hanif Abd Azis. The company was unrepresented.
Based on the facts of the case, the offence was uncovered during an integrated operation conducted by the ministry and the Perak Islamic Religious Department (JAIPk).
Inspections found the premises were selling food products bearing halal logos, although checks by JAIPk revealed the halal logo used on the products was invalid.
The court was told the factory raided during the operation did not possess Malaysian halal certification, although the same company held certification for another factory in Jalan Lintang, Sungai Siput.
In mitigation, the accused said the factory in Jalan Besar Sungai Siput renewed its halal certification 13 days after the raid, on Nov 16, 2025.
The accused also argued the offence involved a technical issue, as the packaging used the address of the company's factory in Jalan Lintang, which possessed Malaysian halal certification.
"After the raid, we immediately stopped production at the factory and corrected the address on the products besides removing the halal logo," said the accused, who is one of the company's three directors.
Apart from the fine, the court also ordered seven types of seized peanut products involving 15 tins and 646 units weighing about 211.6kg to be forfeited to the Malaysian government for disposal.
The accused paid the fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's involvement in BRICS to strengthen global economic position
Article:
SEREMBAN: Malaysia's participation in BRICS is seen as having the potential to strengthen the country's position in global economic competition, as well as reduce dependence on the traditional financial system dominated by major powers, said Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan.
He said the formation of the bloc aims to ensure that countries of the Global South are not continually sidelined, noting that BRICS accounts for about 30 per cent of global gross domestic product (GDP) and represents a significant share of the world's population.
"Our agenda is to ensure that BRICS countries are able to enhance and maximise their capabilities so that they are not left behind, especially in the post-COVID-19 period.
"Furthermore, many countries are burdened with debt, so I believe the global financial system also needs to be reviewed. We no longer want to be overly dependent on a single system," he told reporters after launching the Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Seri Sendayan athletics championship here today.
Mohamad, who is also Rembau MP and Rantau assemblyman, said Malaysia is now expanding the use of local currencies in trade with neighbouring countries, including ASEAN members, to reduce reliance on third-party currencies.
He said, at the same time, efforts are being strengthened to boost trade among member countries and reinforce their respective currencies through regional economic cooperation.
"This is why the ringgit is strengthening, as the country's trade volume has increased significantly. In fact, in the first quarter of this year alone, Malaysia's total trade exceeded RM1 trillion.
"Malaysia is a trading nation. Any effort to enhance national trade must be carried out in an orderly manner. We do not want to be overly exposed to the use of foreign currencies; it is better for us to trade using our own currencies and through our respective central banks," he said.
Meanwhile, Mohamad said Malaysia can play an important role in BRICS given its position as an ASEAN member and an active trading nation at the international level.
He said Malaysia can leverage the principles of multilateralism shared by BRICS and ASEAN to strengthen governance and enhance investor confidence in the country.
He added that Malaysia continues to be a preferred destination for foreign investors due to its clear, consistent and principled foreign policy, including its commitment to neutrality and its firm stance against human rights violations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt strives to secure energy supply security until December
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government is working to ensure that the country's energy supply security can be extended until December, following the global supply crisis triggered by conflicts in the Middle East.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said the nation's energy supply security is currently projected to be sufficient until July, with ongoing efforts to secure additional resources from abroad.
"We want to reduce consumption and encourage energy savings to extend the lifespan of our oil and gas reserves.
"At present, our energy supply security will last until July. Petronas is working tirelessly to secure available supplies, even having to source them from Africa. If all these supplies can be secured, we will ensure our energy supply security until December, he said.
Fadillah, who is also Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said this after officiating the Sustainable Rebate and Incentive Assistance (SuRIA) Home solar rebate initiative at the ministry's monthly assembly here today.
Also present were Deputy Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad and the ministry's secretary-general Datuk Mad Zaidi Mohd Karli.
Fadillah noted that the current global energy crisis has a domino effect on the entire economic chain.
"The impact will become more visible in the second and third phases due to supply shortages, which will affect other sectors like healthcare, as plastic is heavily required for packaging, alongside many other industrial needs.
"The manufacturing sector and others will also be affected, impacting the public as a whole. This is especially true since our country is dealing with massive subsidies that the government must bear to cushion the blow for the rakyat," he said.
He added that the National Economic Action Council (Neac) meets weekly to conduct engagements with various sectors to assess how the crisis impacts the country's economy.
The council also evaluates how the government can help mitigate these effects and ensure business continuity, particularly for small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
"This is what we are implementing, and it requires cooperation from all levels of society to fulfil their responsibilities. For instance, when we practice working from home, we must work diligently at home instead of being outside," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mother might not know Zara Qairina's perception of her, expert says
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Noraidah Lamat, the mother of Zara Qairina Mahathir may have had good intentions when disciplining her daughter but the girl may have perceived it negatively and experienced psychological pain, an expert told the Coroner's Court today.
Lawyer Joan Goh questioned consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung whether Zara Qairina had written in her diary about being scolded over handphone usage, where the teen questioned why her mother had beaten her over it.
Goh: This may have caused her to write, "She's really stupid, damn it, always hitting people's legs. I'm already grown up — do you still think I'm a little kid? So stupid. It's up to me if I want to look at my phone. Who are you to keep stopping me? Idiot!" Agree?
Dr Chua: It is possible.
Goh: Is it possible that her mother did not know how her daughter perceived her actions and intentions?
Dr Chua: It is very possible. Masking, conformity and internalisation are exceedingly common among adolescents with psychological distress. It is inherently elusive even to parents.
Goh: Unless her mother read her diaries?
Dr Chua: Yes.
Goh is representing one of the five children charged with insulting remarks against Zara Qairina.
The inquest will resume next month from June 22 to 26.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: France may consider windfall tax on sectors buoyed by Iran crisis, finance minister says
Article:
PARIS: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure said on Friday the government may consider imposing a windfall tax on companies amid the surge in energy prices during the war in Iran.
"If there are particular companies that have made an exceptional amount, maybe we will impose additional taxes, I'll say it that way, but this debate will take place in the fall," Lescure told Sud Radio.
Several opposition French politicians have advocated for additional so-called windfall taxes on oil companies such as TotalEnergies since the war in Iran started in late February.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor special assembly session on June 24 remains unconfirmed
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Speculation surrounding a special Johor state legislative assembly session on June 24, which has triggered rumours of an early dissolution, remains unconfirmed.
Johor state assembly Deputy Speaker Datuk Samsolbari Jamali briefly said that the matter has yet to be finalised.
"It is not confirmed," he said.
Earlier, Samsolbari, who is also Ayer Hitam Umno division chief, joined 25 other division chiefs for a committee meeting at the Johor Umno liaison office here.
The meeting was chaired by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is the state Umno liaison chairman and Simpang Renggam Umno division chief.
Also present were Johor Umno deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan and state Umno information chief Md Israk Abdullah.
Leaders from the state Umno wings were also in attendance, including Wanita chief Asiah Md Ariff, Pemuda chief Noor Azleen Ambros, and Puteri chief Rina Farhana Abdullah.
Following the meeting, Onn Hafiz declined to hold a press conference and merely smiled at members of the media.
"Let me through," he jested.
An Umno division chief met after the session said the agenda focused entirely on grassroots machinery and community outreach programmes, downplaying any discussions about snap state polls.
"We discussed our machinery's groundwork. There was no mention of election dates," he said.
Other division chiefs tightly held their cards close to their chest, smiling and refusing to comment when approached by reporters.
Speculation regarding an early Johor state
election
has intensified recently following claims that the state assembly would be dissolved ahead of schedule.
During the March 2022 state election, Barisan Nasional formed the state government with a comfortable majority after securing 40 out of the 56 state seats.
The current term of the Johor government is only scheduled to expire in April 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar visits HKL, prays for patients' recovery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid a visit to Hospital Kuala Lumpur (HKL) to observe its operations and meet patients receiving treatment at the facility.
In a Facebook post, Anwar said he also took the opportunity to greet patients during the visit.
"I pray that they are granted recovery, that all their affairs are eased, and that they continue to be blessed with strength," he said.
The prime minister also expressed appreciation to HKL healthcare workers for their continued dedication and commitment in caring for the public.
"Thank you to all HKL healthcare personnel who continue to serve with patience, compassion and immense sacrifice in safeguarding the lives and wellbeing of the people every day," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Authorities investigating viral claims of newborn thrown from 10th floor of PPR flat
Article:
JOHOR BARU:  Authorities are investigating a viral claim involving a newborn baby girl who allegedly died after being thrown from the 10th floor of a People's Housing Project (PPR) flat here.
Johor Women, Family and Community Development Committee chairman Khairin Nisa Ismail said the incident, believed to have occurred several days ago, has sparked widespread public concern.
It is understood that the infant was believed to have been newborn as the umbilical cord was still attached, and the mother is allegedly in her 20s.
"This is a heartbreaking incident, and the cause is still under investigation. I hope the public will give the police space to carry out their probe," she said when contacted.
Meanwhile, a police spokesman confirmed that the incident is currently under investigation.
Based on viral photos and information circulated online, the baby was discovered in the parking lot of the flat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC unveils regulatory codes under Online Safety Act to protect children online
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) today unveiled two regulatory codes under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) to better protect minors from harmful online content.
The Child Protection Code (CPC) and the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC) were published following engagements with industry players, civil society organisations, and relevant stakeholders, including a public consultation held from Feb 12 to March 31.
In a statement, MCMC said both codes will take effect on June 1, adding that a reasonable grace period will be provided for service providers to effectively complete the verification process.
"The codes set clear expectations for service providers to take greater responsibility in addressing online harms within their services, particularly in safeguarding children and vulnerable users.
"The implementation approach under the codes remains outcome-based, allowing service providers the flexibility to adopt appropriate solutions that meet safety, privacy and legal requirements."
The commission said the CPC focuses on ensuring a safer and more age-appropriate online experience for child users by requiring service providers to embed child safety by design principles into their services, including safeguards to limit account registration and ownership by users under the age of 16.
"The measures under CPC provide age-appropriate protections and restrictions on high-risk features, while helping to reduce exposure to exploitative interactions and harmful content."
As for the RMC, the commission said it requires service providers to implement proactive and systemic measures to mitigate harmful content risks, including risk assessments, stronger content governance, effective reporting and response mechanisms, advertiser verification measures and the labelling of manipulated content where appropriate.
"Both CPC and RMC constitute an important step in strengthening Malaysia's approach to online safety. The implementation of these key obligations under ONSA also forms part of the government's ongoing efforts to ensure a safer digital experience for children and families.
"These measures help strengthen the protection of children in the online environment, while providing added reassurance to parents in navigating increasingly complex digital risks."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High Court rejects application to freeze RM548mil assets linked to Daim's wife
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has rejected an application by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to secure an access restriction order against RM548 million worth of assets allegedly linked to Toh Puan Na'imah Abdul Khalid, the wife of the late former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin, her son, and seven other parties.
Judge Datuk Mohd Arief Emran Arifin made the ruling after finding that the prosecution failed to satisfy the statutory requirements under Section 53 of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (Amlatfpuaa 2001).
"The threshold of proof required under Section 53 of Amlatfpuaa has not been met. Therefore, the application is rejected," he said when delivering the judgment.
MACC deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat appeared for the prosecution, while Na'imah, her son, and the seven other parties were represented by counsel Datuk Dr Gurdial Singh Nijar.
On June 19 last year, the prosecution filed an ex-parte application to restrain Na'imah, her son, Muhammed Amir Zainuddin Daim, two individuals, five companies, or any related parties from dealing with properties located outside Malaysia.
The offshore assets comprise investment funds totalling GBP21 million (about RM121 million) and US$99 million (about RM423 million) held in Singapore.
The application was filed under Section 53 of Amlatfpuaa 2001 following investigations conducted under Section 113 of the Income Tax Act and Section 4(1) of Amlatfpuaa.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man found dead in drain died of heart disease, no foul play suspected
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police have ruled out foul play in the death of a man whose body was found in a drain beside a shop at Jalan Kedah here yesterday.
Northeast district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said a post-mortem conducted at the Penang Hospital Forensic Department today found that the cause of death was Ischemic Heart Disease.
"The post-mortem results showed that the victim died due to blocked heart blood vessels (Ischemic Heart Disease).
"There were no criminal elements involved," he said in a statement today.
It was reported that the body of the 37-year-old local man was found in a drain beside a row of shophouses along Jalan Kedah here yesterday.
Passersby who discovered the
man unconscious
in the drain alerted police to the incident.
Investigations at the scene identified the deceased as a man from Kapar, Selangor.
Police believe the victim was homeless and had been staying in the vicinity where he was found.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM20,000 debt, love dispute sparked viral Sentul Raya brawl
Article:
austin@nst.com.my
KUALA LUMPUR: A RM20,000 debt and a romantic dispute are believed to have been the spark behind a viral brawl involving several individuals in Sentul Raya here on Wednesday.
Sentul deputy police chief Superintendent Jasni Zolpa said police have so far arrested four local suspects comprising two men and two women.
He said investigations revealed the altercation stemmed from personal issues and unpaid debts among individuals who knew each other.
"The fight was caused by personal problems and debt issues. All four suspects have been remanded for two days beginning yesterday and the remand ends today.
"Three more suspects have been identified and are still at large, but efforts are underway to track them down as soon as possible.
"All the suspects know each other. They are friends and have personal relationships," he told reporters here today.
Yesterday, police
arrested four people
after a violent altercation in Sentul was caught on video and circulated on social media.
Investigations were being conducted after a police report was lodged.
The video, which has gone viral online, showed several men involved in a scuffle.
In the footage, a group of people can be seen shouting and exchanging blows, while one man appears to be pinned to the ground during the commotion.
Several vehicles and motorcycles were also seen surrounding the area as onlookers watched the incident unfold.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hostel interrogation one of the main factors which led to Zara's suicide, expert says
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The interrogation of Zara Qairina Mahathir by her seniors was one of the main factors that led to the 13-year-old found lying unconscious outside her dormitory on July 16 last year, an expert told the Coroner's court.
Consultant Forensic Psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung testified that the interrogation took place when Zara Qairina was called to A-3-6 dormitory between 9pm and midnight on July 15.
She was later found unconscious on the ground floor of SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha around 3am.
"The deceased had multiple predisposing suicide risk factors. It was inadequate for a suicide to occur without direct factors such as the interrogation in (dormitory room) A-3-6," the 67th witness testified before coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the last day of week-long inquest into the 13-year-old girl's death.
During proceedings, the expert told the court that Zara Qairina's stressors included disappointment related to becoming a badar (prefect),
unsatisfactory academic achievement and chronic insufficient sleep among others
.
Lawyer Abdul Fikry Jaafar Abdullah questioned whether Zara Qairina committed suicide immediately after the confrontation as there was  a gap of between three and four hours before she was found unconscious.
"It has to be noted that being physically and psychologically isolated, a person who has problems regulating her emotions would likely engage in other emotionally focused strategies including ruminations.
"Together with the cognitive restriction, it created a sense of no alternative and inescapability.
"That led to the event of eventual suicide, I did not see any other factors in between those hours other than the interrogations," Dr Chua said.
Fikry is representing four of the children accused of hurling insulting remarks against Zara Qairina in a separate trial.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jamal Yunos issues public apology to Khairy, settles defamation suit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The defamation suit filed by former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin against Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos was resolved today after the Sungai Besar Umno division chief issued a public apology before the High Court here.
Khairy initiated the legal action on May 24, 2023, following defamatory remarks made during a media statement at the 2022 Umno General Assembly on Jan 14 that year.
Reading the draft consent judgment, Jamal admitted that his allegations against the former Rembau member of parliament were false, unfounded, and lacked sufficient evidence.
"I sincerely regret the publication and dissemination of the said statements, which have caused distress, public contempt, and injury to Khairy's reputation, character, and credibility.
"Accordingly, I hereby retract fully all defamatory statements I issued against Khairy during the media conference and in the said publication," he said.
Jamal admitted he had falsely alleged that Khairy hired or paid delegates to heckle speakers, and had implied the former Rembau MP was corrupt and lacked integrity.
"I acknowledge that my statements during the media conference and in the said publication bore the meaning or imputation that Khairy is a person lacking integrity, is unfit as a leader, and was involved in corrupt practices, all of which are wholly false and without foundation.
"Accordingly, I hereby agree to immediately and permanently remove the video recording of the said media conference and any form of publication from the Facebook account 'DS Jamal Yunos (DS Jamal Yunos Official)' and the TikTok account 'jamal_yunos', and to ensure that such publications are no longer accessible to the public," he stated.
Jamal further undertook not to repeat or republish any similar or equivalent statements against Khairy in the future.
Judge Datuk Anand Ponnudurai thereafter recorded a consent judgment in terms of the settlement set out in the draft judgment.
Earlier, Khairy's counsel, Harshaan Muhammad Zamani, informed the court that both parties had agreed to settle the matter based on the reached resolution.
This was confirmed by lawyers Mohammed Nasser Yusof and Mohamad Fauzi Abdul Samad, who acted for Jamal.
At today's proceedings, Harshaan was assisted by counsel Shakeel Mohd Saifuzzaman, Muhamad Nur Aizat Noor Azam, and Sheetal Kaur Arwinder Singh.
According to the statement of claim, Khairy alleged that Jamal had convened a media conference at the Kuala Lumpur World Trade Centre, which was attended by media practitioners, Umno delegates, and several party members and leaders during the assembly on Jan 14, 2023.
He further alleged that on the same day, Jamal uploaded a defamatory video recording titled "I hope Khairy Jamaluddin is disciplined by the Umno Disciplinary Committee after issuing careless and embarrassing statements about Umno #UmnoDuluKiniDanSelamanya" on his Facebook and TikTok accounts.
Khairy claimed that the defamatory statements bore the imputation, among others, that he was a leader devoid of integrity, unfit for leadership, and disloyal to Umno and its party leadership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor assembly dissolution absolute prerogative of the King: Mohamad Hasan
Article:
SEREMBAN: The dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly to pave the way for a snap state election is the absolute prerogative of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, says Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan.
He said the coalition remains confident that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi will advise Sultan Ibrahim, who is the Sultan of Johor, on the appropriate timing for the state polls.
"It could be soon, it could be later, that depends on the menteri besar's discretion. So far, however, there have been no official discussions at the party headquarters or central leadership level.
"I am confident that once the menteri besar receives the green light or royal consent, he will consult the central leadership so that preparations for the state election can be planned together," he told reporters after officiating the SMK Seri Sendayan Athletics Championship here today.
On May 16, Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor BN chairman, announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election. In the 2022 state polls, BN won 40 seats, while Pakatan Harapan secured 12, Perikatan Nasional three, and Muda one.
Meanwhile, Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, reminded all parties to handle political developments maturely and prudently to safeguard public well-being.
"We do not want the people to feel uneasy. All decisions or actions must be done properly because our purpose as a government is to help the people, not to burden them," he said.
In a separate development, Mohamad, who is also the Rembau member of parliament and Rantau assemblyman, said the government has approved the construction of two new schools in Bandar Sri Sendayan to address overcrowding.
He said the projects, involving a secondary and primary school, have entered the tender stage with a total allocation of about RM135 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MSM names Mazlan Kamaruddin as new chairman
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MSM Malaysia Holdings Bhd has appointed Datuk Mazlan Kamaruddin as its chairman effective immediately.
He succeeded Datuk Syed Hisham Syed Wazir, who stepped down earlier this month after completing his maximum nine-year tenure on the board.
Mazlan, 68, brings more than three decades of experience in advisory, corporate planning and strategic consulting.
According to a Bursa Malaysia filing, Mazlan is currently senior adviser at Ernst & Young Advisory Services Sdn Bhd, a role he has held since 2017 after retiring as partner at the firm.
Prior to that, he served as partner at Ernst & Young from 2002 to 2017 and at Arthur Andersen/Hanafiah Raslan Mohamad Management Services from 1990 to 2002.
He also previously worked at Malayan Banking Bhd, then known as United Malayan Banking Corp Bhd, in corporate planning and investments between 1985 and 1990.
Mazlan currently sits on the boards of several companies within the Naza Group ecosystem including Naza TTDI Sdn Bhd, Naza Automotive Holdings Sdn Bhd and Naza Motor Trading Sdn Bhd as an independent non-executive director.
He holds a Master of Business Administration from Morehead State University, a degree in Business Administration (Marketing) from Western Michigan University and a diploma in business studies from Universiti Teknologi Mara.
The appointment comes amid broader leadership changes at MSM, including the appointment of Dr Mazatul 'Aini Shahar Abdul Malek Shahar as group chief executive officer in February 2026 as the company intensifies efforts to strengthen operations and corporate governance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Family members will find it difficult to accept Zara committed suicide, court hears
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Family members would find difficult to accept that Zara Qairina Mahathir had committed suicide as she had not shown any signs of distress, the court heard.
On the 72nd day of inquest, lawyer Abdul Fikry Jaafar Abdullah questioned Consultant Forensic Psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung on his conclusion that Zara Qairina's manner of
death was consistent with suicide
.
Dr Chua said Zara Qairina would have had serious emotional and psychological distress, despite others describing her as a cheerful person.
The expert said those with suicidal tendencies may hide or mask their true feelings to avoid showing signs of distress so as to not worry or alarm those around them, particularly family members.
Dr Chua, however, highlighted there was non-suicidal self-injury (NSSI) and the "barcode" injuries, as signs and indicators of emotional distress or psychological distress.
In previous proceedings, the court was told by several witnesses that Zara Qairina had shown
these "barcode" markings to others
.
Fikry: When the patient has suicidal tendencies, it is common for the family members to later experience shock, denial, or disbelief upon learning that the patient had committed suicide. Would you agree?
Dr Chua:
Agree.
Fikry: Family members would not been aware of the patient's internal struggles or suicidal thoughts and would later find it difficult to accept that the patient had taken their own life. Would you agree?
Dr Chua:
Agree.
Fikry: The reaction of denial or disbelief from family members is not unusual in suicide cases especially when the deceased appeared normal or happy before the death. Would you agree?
Dr Chua:
Agree.
Fikry: The family's difficulty in accepting the possibility of suicide does not by itself disprove the forensic or psychological findings consistent with suicide. Do you agree?
Dr Chua:
Agree.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Critical Holdings unit bags RM152.6mil contract from "renowned American MNC'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Critical Holdings Bhd's unit Critical Engineering Solutions Sdn Bhd has secured a RM152.6 million engineering, procurement and construction contract for cleanroom and utilities works at Kulim Hi-Tech Park in Kedah.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the contract was awarded by a "renowned US-based multinational corporation" that supplies wafer fabrication equipment and services to the semiconductor industry.
The contract covers the construction of a new cleanroom architecture associated with mechanical, electrical and process utilities services at the industrial park.
The works commenced on May 11 and are scheduled for completion by Dec 25, 2026, the company said.
Critical Holdings said the contract is expected to contribute to the company's earnings and net assets for the financial year ending June 30, 2027.
It that none of its directors, major shareholders or persons connected to them have any direct or indirect interest in the contract.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARC Ratings affirms stable outlook on CIMB, CIMB Islamic
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MARC Ratings has affirmed a stable outlook on its ratings for CIMB Group Bhd and CIMB Islamic Bank Bhd.
The agency affirmed its corporate credit ratings on CIMB at AA+/MARC-1 and the AA rating on the bank's RM10 billion Basel III–compliant Tier 2 subordinated debt programme.
The firm said that CIMB's capitalisation remained strong, with key ratios well above regulatory requirements.
"The group's funding profile is sound, supported by a stable funding ratio of 83.8 per cent and a healthy current and savings account (CASA) ratio of 46.2 per cent in 2025. While deposit competition is expected to keep funding costs elevated, continued emphasis on CASA growth should help mitigate cost pressures," it said in a statement.
CIMB's total assets stood at RM778.7 billion as at end-2025.
"The group's designation as a domestic systemically important bank by Bank Negara Malaysia underscores its importance to the financial system."
It noted that the bank's loan growth moderated to 0.2 per cent in 2025, reflecting a more selective lending stance and weaker macroeconomic conditions in overseas markets.
The strengthening ringgit further weighed on growth, particularly in Indonesia and Thailand.
Nonetheless, MARC said Malaysian operations remained resilient, recording 4.2 per cent growth, supported by the consumer and commercial segments.
"Asset quality improved steadily over the past five years, with the gross impaired loans ratio declining to 1.7 per cent in 2025, reflecting broad-based improvement across operating markets. Loan loss coverage increased to 134 per cent, notwithstanding a reduction in management overlays to RM984 million.
"Despite external headwinds from the Middle East conflict, asset quality is expected to remain resilient, supported by a predominantly retail loan mix (55.4 per cent) and moderate SME exposure (13.2 per cent)," it added.
Separately, MARC affirmed its financial institution ratings for CIMB Islamic at AAA/MARC-1 with a stable outlook.
The agency also affirmed its ratings on CIMB Islamic's sukuk programmes namely at AAAIS/Stable for the RM10 billion senior sukuk Wakalah programme and at AA+IS/Stable for the bank's RM5 billion Tier 2 junior sukuk programme.
The firm said that CIMB Islamic remains the second-largest full-fledged Islamic bank in Malaysia, with total assets of RM188.9 billion as of end-2025, representing a 14.9 per cent share of domestic Islamic banking assets.
Financing growth accelerated to nine per cent, surpassing the industry average of 7.9 per cent, underpinned by continued expansion in both the retail and corporate segments.
Amid Middle East–related headwinds, MARC said the bank's portfolio is expected to remain resilient, supported by its largely retail mix (67.8 per cent) and moderate SME exposure (17.3 per cent).
"Financing loss coverage stands above the Islamic banking industry average at 142.4 per cent, providing a buffer against potential asset quality pressures from energy price volatility, supply chain disruptions and softer business sentiment," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hibiscus Petroleum swings back to profit in Q3
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd returned to the black in the third quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q3 FY26), supported by higher oil prices.
The company posted a net profit of RM80.1 million, reversing from a net loss of RM116 million a year earlier, lifted by a 12 per cent quarter-on-quarter rise in its average realised oil and condensate price to US$76.7 per barrel.
However, revenue slipped 9.6 per cent to RM517.8 million from RM572.8 million a year ago, according to a bourse filing.
Hibiscus Petroleum also declared its third interim single-tier dividend of three sen per share that will be paid on July 17.
For the nine-month period, the company's net profit nearly fourfold to RM170.77 million from RM42.9 million while revenue fell to RM1.5 billion from RM1.7 billion in the same period last year.
Managing director Datuk Dr Kenneth Pereira said Hibiscus Petroleum is on track operationally, with the start-up of the Brunei LPC project and continued progress towards first oil from the Teal West Development by mid-2026.
He said this supports the company's net production target of 35,000 barrels of oil equivalent  daily day by end-2026.
"Strong oil prices and favourable oil premiums are expected to support continued earnings and cash flow momentum into the fourth quarter, enabling us to deliver enhanced shareholder returns.
"This includes our targeted FY26 dividend total of 10 sen per share, which would be a record high for a financial year.
"Looking ahead, we continue to advance projects to increase production while engaging with reputable potential strategic investors to further unlock long-term value for shareholders," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas reviews Bersatu's conduct
Article:
MARANG: Pas has not set a deadline for Bersatu to clarify its actions, which allegedly violate the spirit of camaraderie within Perikatan Nasional (PN).
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, however, said the party's Syura Ulama Council and election department have been tasked with examining every negative action taken by Bersatu previously.
"Most recently, we sent a letter to Bersatu regarding the situation in Negri Sembilan, and other matters will be discussed internally by Pas.
"We have also raised the same issues during past PN meetings," he said at a press conference here today.
Hadi, who is also the Marang member of parliament, said the recent moves by Pas would not hinder PN's preparations for any upcoming elections.
"Pas remains unaffected, and we are ready to face an election at any time, he said.
Meanwhile, Hadi said Pas is prepared to consider the participation of Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's new political entity in the PN coalition.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ‘Balik kampung’ rush begins at Kuala Lumpur transport hubs as travellers try to beat Aidiladha crowds
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Major public transport terminals around the capital are seeing a surge in travellers heading back to their hometowns ahead of the long holiday and upcoming Hari Raya Aidiladha celebration.
A Bernama survey found increasing crowds at key terminals, including Terminal Bersepadu Gombak (TBG), Terminal Bersepadu Selatan (TBS) and KL Sentral, as passengers began making early travel preparations to avoid last-minute congestion.
A TBG representative, Mohd Raimi Jusoh, said ticket sales for the festive season opened two months ago to give passengers more time to plan their journeys.
He said daily ticket sales at TBG, which usually average about 1,000, have surged to around 7,000 during the long holiday and Aidiladha period.
“There has been a sharp increase in ticket sales and bus trips as Aidiladha coincides with the long school holidays.
“TBG is widely known as a gateway to the East Coast, with 70 to 80 per cent of bus operations serving destinations there, although the terminal also caters to other routes,” he told Bernama today.
To cope with the increased demand, Mohd Raimi said daily bus trips had been raised to between 200 and 210 from about 150 on normal days, following approval from the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD).
He said the two-week school holidays had also allowed travellers to spread out their journeys and avoid the usual last-minute rush seen during festive periods.
TBG management has also provided 14 special buses in collaboration with several boarding schools in Pahang, Melaka and Negeri Sembilan to facilitate students’ travel.
Meanwhile, passenger Muhammad Aiman Nor Hakim, 25, said he purchased his ticket two weeks earlier through a bus booking application to avoid ticket shortages and higher fares.
“I live in Setapak and will return to Bachok, Kelantan for Aidiladha with my family. Booking early is cheaper because ticket prices usually increase closer to the festive season,” said the first-time TBG bus user.
Meanwhile, TMAS Sdn Bhd, the operator of TBS, said ticket sales at the terminal began rising since May 22 and are expected to remain high until June 7.
A company representative, who declined to be named, said about 50 additional buses had been deployed for various destinations based on routes approved by APAD.
“Overall, ticket sales have increased by about 20 per cent compared with last year’s festive season, driven by Aidiladha and the school holidays,” she said.
The company has also carried out system upgrades, scheduled maintenance and increased manpower to ensure smooth operations during the peak travel period.
At KL Sentral, many electric train service (ETS) passengers were also seen booking tickets early for their journeys home.
Passenger Zanariyah Awang, 60, hoped transport operators would add more train coaches to accommodate the increase in travellers during the festive season. — Bernama

Title: No other trigger linked to Zara Qairina’s death besides dorm interrogation, inquest told
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 22 — A consultant forensic psychiatrist told the Coroner’s Court here today that he disagreed that the late Zara Qairina Mahathir was very likely to have committed suicide even without the confrontation at her seniors’ dorm.
Dr Chua Sze Hung testified before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan that suicide risk factors can improve or worsen.
“It is dynamic and it would be hypothetical,” the witness said under examination-in-chief by counsel Abdul Fikry Jaafar Abdullah.
Abdul Fikry: Without the confrontation there is a possibility Zara may have committed suicide due to other triggers or destabilising factors, correct?
Dr Chua: I disagree, I did not find any other trigger or destabilising factor on that night or morning other than the issue in the said dorm.
Abdul Fikry: Before the confrontation, Zara had exhibited predominant suicide risk factors?
Dr Chua: Yes, Zara had multiple predisposing suicide risk factors but it was inadequate for a suicide to occur without direct factors such as the interrogation in the said dorm.
Abdul Fikry: Based on your materials you have reviewed, what is the estimated time of the confrontation in the said dorm?
Dr Chua: It was after the return to the hostel which was after 9pm onwards and before 12 midnight.
Abdul Fikry: And what is the estimated time when Zara was found lying on the ground?
Dr Chua: If I remember correctly was roughly around 3am.
Abdul Fikry: Based on the timing, can you tell the court, how long is the estimated time between the confrontation and the time when Zara was found lying on the ground?
Dr Chua: It was probably around three to four hours.
Abdul Fikry: Based on your findings, your conclusion, was the manner of death consistent with suicide, correct?
Dr Chua: Correct.
Abdul Fikry: According to the timeline, Zara did not immediately commit suicide after the confrontation, correct?
Dr Chua: According to the timeline, after the confrontation, Zara returned back to her dorm. She was crying profusely in a manner her friends had not seen before. She was in profoundly stressed following the interrogation. She then approached a few of her friends, namely A22, A23, and A24 asking for a (pair of) scissors before leaving the room. She was then noted to be in the toilet by A25, A26, and A27. The nearest most direct factor identified at this point is the confrontation had to be noted that being physically and psychological isolated a person, who has problem regulating her emotions, would likely engage in other emotionally focussed strategies including rumination together with the cognitive restriction it created a sense of no alternative and in ‘escapability’ that led to the eventual suicide. I didn’t see any other factors in between those hours other than the interrogation.
Abdul Fikry: However, the confrontation had long ended before Zara was found lying on the ground? Agree?
Dr Chua: One has to be aware that an adolescent, who has emotional regulation issue, rejection sensitivity, the experience would have been perceived more narratively and the pain more intensely. Ruminating on it continues the pain and exacerbates the pain, making the pain last long after the confrontation.
Abdul Fikry is one of the lawyers representing child offenders with the pseudonyms Students B, C, D, and E.
Meanwhile, Dr Chua stated Zara Qairina’s case is his first psychological autopsy.
He said his previous experience in court was to testify in, for example, insanity cases.
Under examination-in-chief by counsel Datuk Rizwandean Bukhary M Borhan, the 67th witness said he did not recall testifying in cases involving adolescents.
The inquest resumes on June 22.
Zara Qairina, 13, was found under her dormitory building on July 16, 2025 and died the next day in hospital.
Her remains were exhumed on August 9, 2025 and a postmortem was performed on August 10 at the Department of Forensic Medicine, Queen Elizabeth Hospital.
On August 20, 2025, five minors, who were give the pseudonyms Student A, B, C, D, and E, were charged with using insulting words against Zara Qairina. — The Borneo Post

Title: Three Myanmar nationals remanded over compatriot’s death in Kepong
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Three Myanmar nationals, including a woman, have been remanded for seven days until Monday to assist in the investigation into the death of a fellow countryman in Kepong.
Sentul deputy police chief Supt Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 30 and 40, were arrested last Monday.
“Initial investigations found that the incident was believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding that led to a fight between the victim and all the suspects, who were intoxicated, resulting in the victim’s death,” he said at a press conference at the Sentul district police headquarters today.
He said the body of the 39-year-old victim was discovered under the MRT Metro Prima bridge along Jalan Metro Perdana Barat 1, Kepong, by members of the public at about 1.40pm last Sunday, and the case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
In another development, Jasni said police detained four individuals — one man and three women — to assist in investigations into a fight in Sentul believed to have been caused by personal disputes and debt issues.
“All the suspects were remanded for two days, and the remand period ends today. Police have also identified three more suspects, and efforts to track them down and arrest them are ongoing,” he said, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 148 of the Penal Code. — Bernama

Title: MMEA detains over 4,000 undocumented migrants in first four months of 2026
Article:
SANDAKAN, May 22 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained 4,107 illegal immigrants and 316 smugglers nationwide in the first four months of this year.
MMEA deputy director-general of Operations Vice Admiral Mohd Zawawi Abdullah said migrant smuggling syndicates remain active and exploit the country’s waters due to Malaysia’s strategic position in the region.
“Statistics from January to April show that the highest number of detained illegal immigrants came from three countries, namely Myanmar (1,932), Indonesia (1,727), and the Philippines (180), which proves that the threat remains serious.
“This situation requires aggressive and continuous enforcement action by authorities across national waters,” he told reporters after the closing ceremony of Operation Redback 22/2026 (Op Redback) here today.
Mohd Zawawi said MMEA has opened eight investigation papers related to migrant smuggling cases involving 19 smugglers and 233 illegal immigrants.
“Of the total, the majority of detained smugglers were from Indonesia (13), followed by Myanmar (three), Thailand (two), and one local,” he said.
Meanwhile, he said Op Redback has been conducted in collaboration with the Australian Border Force (ABF) since May 18 in Sandakan waters, and the operation reflects ongoing maritime enforcement cooperation between Malaysia and Australia since 2014.
“The operation carried out by MMEA and ABF focuses on combating cross-border crimes, including migrant smuggling, smuggling of controlled goods, search and rescue efforts at sea, as well as Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated (IUU) fishing,” he said.
Mohd Zawawi said the 4,107 arrests made this year clearly demonstrate the effectiveness of strategic cooperation, including with the ABF, and that enforcement efforts must continue in a more aggressive and integrated manner.
He added that there will be no compromise on any criminal activities at sea to safeguard the sovereignty of the nation’s waters. — Bernama

Title: Eighteen-year-old alleged gang leader among six arrested in Sentul motorcycle theft crackdown
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Police have crippled a motorcycle theft syndicate known as ‘Geng Hadi’ following the arrest of six local men, including its 18-year-old leader, in a series of raids in Kepong last Saturday, leading to the resolution of eight theft cases in Sentul involving estimated losses of RM20,000.
Sentul deputy police chief Supt Jasni Zolpa said the suspects, aged between 15 and 33, were detained during an operation conducted by the D4 Branch of the Criminal Investigation Division at the Sentul district police headquarters (IPD).
“The group leader is known as Hadi, while the underage suspect was found to have dropped out of school. All the suspects have previous records related to motorcycle theft cases,” he told a press conference at the Sentul IPD here today.
He said preliminary investigations had found that the group had been active since early this year and was believed to have carried out multiple motorcycle thefts within the Sentul district before dismantling the stolen vehicles and disposing of evidence.
“The syndicate targeted popular motorcycle models including Honda EX5, Modenas and Yamaha RXZ before stripping the vehicles for parts to be sold to third parties or used by the syndicate members,” he said.
Jasni said the dismantling activities were carried out at an empty store in Kepong, with the stripped motorcycle frames later dumped into a nearby river in an attempt to dispose of evidence.
He said during the raids, police seized two motorcycles, five incomplete motorcycle frames, one set of motorcycle components and two mobile phones.
According to him, the arrests and seizures enabled police to solve eight motorcycle theft cases reported in Sentul.
All the suspects had been ordered to be remanded for six days from last Sunday to assist investigations under Section 379A of the Penal Code. — Bernama

Title: Mohamad Hasan says Brics offers Malaysia stronger footing in global economy
Article:
SEREMBAN, May 22 — Malaysia’s participation in Brics is seen as having the potential to strengthen the country’s position in global economic competition, as well as reduce dependence on the traditional financial system dominated by major powers, said Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan.
He said the formation of the bloc aims to ensure that countries of the Global South are not continually sidelined, noting that Brics accounts for about 30 per cent of global gross domestic product (GDP) and represents a significant share of the world’s population.
“Our agenda is to ensure that Brics countries are able to enhance and maximise their capabilities so that they are not left behind, especially in the post-Covid-19 period.
“Furthermore, many countries are burdened with debt, so I believe the global financial system also needs to be reviewed. We no longer want to be overly dependent on a single system,” he told reporters after launching the Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Seri Sendayan athletics championship here today.
Mohamad, who is also Rembau MP and Rantau assemblyman, said Malaysia is now expanding the use of local currencies in trade with neighbouring countries, including Asean members, to reduce reliance on third-party currencies.
He said, at the same time, efforts are being strengthened to boost trade among member countries and reinforce their respective currencies through regional economic cooperation.
“This is why the ringgit is strengthening, as the country’s trade volume has increased significantly. In fact, in the first quarter of this year alone, Malaysia’s total trade exceeded RM1 trillion.
“Malaysia is a trading nation. Any effort to enhance national trade must be carried out in an orderly manner. We do not want to be overly exposed to the use of foreign currencies; it is better for us to trade using our own currencies and through our respective central banks,” he said.
Meanwhile, Mohamad said Malaysia can play an important role in Brics given its position as an Asean member and an active trading nation at the international level.
He said Malaysia can leverage the principles of multilateralism shared by Brics and Asean to strengthen governance and enhance investor confidence in the country.
He added that Malaysia continues to be a preferred destination for foreign investors due to its clear, consistent and principled foreign policy, including its commitment to neutrality and its firm stance against human rights violations. — Bernama

Title: Prisons Dept: Inmates may receive family visits on May 29–30 for Aidiladha
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Families of inmates at all prison institutions, including Moral Rehabilitation Centres, Special Rehabilitation Centres, Special Detention Facilities and Henry Gurney Schools, are allowed to visit them for the Aidiladha celebration on the third and fourth days of the festive period.
The Prisons Department, in a statement, said that visiting hours have been set from 8.15 am to 4.15 pm, on May 29 and 30, with priority given to Muslim inmates.
“Visitors are not allowed to bring food and drinks for inmates. Instead, these may be purchased at the prison canteen,” it said.
In addition, visitors are discouraged from giving money to inmates as all basic necessities have been provided, except for certain matters such as examination fee payments, which require them to obtain an official PPT 28 receipt from the prison authorities as proof of payment.
It added that visitors are subject to the Prison Act and Prison Regulations, which allow prison authorities to conduct physical inspections upon entry to and exit from the institutions.
Meanwhile, for families unable to visit the prisons in person, online meetings will also be made available for three days from May 30 to June 1.
Families seeking further information may contact the relevant institutions. — Bernama

Title: Dissolution of Johor state assembly is Sultan Ibrahim’s absolute prerogative, says Tok Mat
Article:
SEREMBAN, May 22 — The dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly to pave the way for a state election is the absolute prerogative of the King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, according to Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan.
He said the party was confident that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi would advise Sultan Ibrahim, who is the Sultan of Johor, on the appropriate timing for the state election.
“It could be soon, it could be later, that depends on the Menteri Besar’s discretion. So far, however, there have been no official discussions at the party headquarters or central leadership level.
“I am confident that once the Menteri Besar receives the green light or royal consent, he will consult the central leadership so that preparations for the state election can be planned together,” he told reporters after officiating the SMK Seri Sendayan Athletics Championship, here today.
On May 16, Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor BN chairman, announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election. In the 2022 state polls, BN won 40 seats, while Pakatan Harapan secured 12, Perikatan Nasional three and MUDA one.
Meanwhile, the Umno deputy president also reminded all parties to handle any political turmoil maturely and prudently to ensure that the well-being of the people continues to be preserved.
